title: Operations Supervisor - Global Logistics Company
description:
Position Summary

This position is responsible for leading a team in the provision of logistics operational management.


Responsibilities

Primary Responsibilities

• Assist the Assistant Manager, Operations in running and overseeing the smooth running of day-to-day operations.

• Lead the team to achieve the department's KPI goals.

• Prepare, maintain and update documentation of processes to meet accounts requirement and changes in customer requirements.

• To ensure work processes are in compliance with documented procedures and proper housekeeping of the warehouse.

• Ensure timely processing of all inbound and outbound activities.

• Ensures the highest degree of efficiency is maintained at all times.

• Supervises and monitors the performance of employees to ensure standard practices and proper care of using the equipment, materials and supplies in the warehouse.

• Provide on-the-job training to warehouse staff.

• Perform inventory reconciliation on stock discrepancy.

• Update WMS on stocks return and follow-up system updates of closeout items.

• Manage inventory accuracy via planned cycle count, monthly inventory reconciliation, inventory analysis and related reports.

• Highlight to Operations Manager for any irregularity in the warehouse.

• Ensure warehouse operations safety and security.

• Assist the Operations Manager in implementation of work improvement projects.

• Communicates with customer on operations and service issues.

• Other duties as assigned by Operations Manager.


Position-specific Responsibilities

• Required to work on bonded license operation


Qualification Summary

Experience:

This position requires minimum 5 years of relevant experience in Logistics with at least 3 years in supervisory position.

Education:

'O' levels or Diploma in Logistics, Engineering or Business Administration

Skills:

• Strong organizational and time management skills; specifically documentation of all issues, communication with customers and transporters.

• Excellent knowledge in Warehouse Management System (WMS) and able to generate and analyze inventory reports.

• Good to have experience in Tradenet, DG handling, Lean system & ISO.

• Good understanding Customer requirements for each account

• Proficient in MS Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Outlook etc

• Good leadership skills

• Able to use systematic approach in decision-making.

• Exhibit good judgment and understanding of issues.

Working hours:

Mon to Fri: 8:30am to 6pm

Company Overview

If you’re seeking for employment or employees, you’re at the right place.Welcome to Flintex Consulting, a leading Recruitment Agency in Singapore.

Our wide industry expertise and select client base enable us to foster fruitful careers rather than short-term jobs and placements.Our advanced placement platform is designed to match employee skill sets with current vacancies with perfect accuracy. Candidates are mentored towards finding the best-fit jobs to help build successful careers.We cover all levels of placement, from entry level professionals to mid-level and CEO level posts.We promise to provide the best suited jobs through our research-based methodologies merged with our top qualified personnel.

Additionally, we offer valuable insights and other personalized services designed to maximize productivity and results when it comes to job search.
